conditions.today: 6 AM—This is not a test. The Emergency Pow-Cast System has been activated following 24 hours of wild weather that has slathered the mountainside with 14 inches of fresh snow...so far. All vertically inclined citizens are being asked to gather their burliest storm-skiing gear and get out here, as wind-driven powder and low-elevation liquid are expected to blast the Tetons through the day. Mild high temperatures will tickle the 30-degree mark at the summit and approach 40 degrees down in the valley. Instead of cheeky rhyming, today I'm going to do a little reminding. Pack some extra patience in your pack, as ski patrol will be working hard to get you safely into the goods as quickly as possible. Heavy accumulations and these strong west-southwest winds are obviously a blessing, but they can also be a bit of a curse, as delays and lift closures may be necessary. Just enjoy the elevated atmospheric energy and be ready for an alpine experience that's extraordinary. -Lege
conditions.yesterday: 6 AM—Pssst, hey. You'd better get up because it's time to get down on an energetic day of midweek storm skiing. Early Tuesday morning on Rendezvous Mountain features a little one-inch topping overnight, and an end to the fresh accumulations is nowhere in sight. Warm high temperatures will reach the mid- to upper 20s at the summit and may produce some liquid precipitation down in the Village as it tickles the upper 30s. Strong west winds in the 10-40 mph range will accelerate the healing on the hill, as every nook and cranny is continually refilled. You can chalk it up to instinct or even some kind of mystical intuition, but whatever you do, listen to the inner voice urging you to spend the day on a personal deep powder mission. -Lege
